ordered_steps.name: build_assurance_input_index
ordered_steps.required_input: compiler review decision, compiler output refs, schema refs, validator refs, QA refs, and trace refs

ordered_steps.name: validate_test_obligations
ordered_steps.required_input: trace row set and test obligation matrix

ordered_steps.name: validate_implementation_workpack_scope
ordered_steps.required_input: implementation workpack index and implementation workpack template

ordered_steps.name: validate_skill_binding_subordination
ordered_steps.required_input: skill binding manifest and skill source text

ordered_steps.name: validate_traceability_exceptions
ordered_steps.required_input: traceability exception ledger and human gate decisions

ordered_steps.name: prepare_final_review_packet
ordered_steps.required_input: final assurance report and assurance input index

assurance_rules: Assurance may inspect only approved compiler and traceability evidence.
Assurance cannot add product, interaction, presentation, layout, compiler, or copy semantics.
Every implementation duty must have one focused trace row.
A trace row must not bundle independent duties or multiple validator checks.
Every trace row must have a matching test obligation row.
Every test obligation row must include both positive and negative cases.
Code targets must be inside allowed write paths and outside forbidden write paths.
Dependency edges must use declared edge types and must drive invalidation.
Implementation workpacks cannot broaden scope beyond trace rows and allowed paths.
generated_at values in reviewed indexes must be deterministic from reviewed release identity, assurance run id, or input-bundle-derived generation id.
Skill bindings remain subordinate to specs, validators, locks, review gates, and source authority.
Traceability exceptions require a human gate decision and explicit scope delta.
Read-only QA may write only READ_ONLY_QA_REPORT.md and qa_finding_index.json.
Read-only QA must not mutate final DRD, manifests, indexes, review decisions, source snapshots, or locks.
Final assurance blocks promotion when any unresolved finding, invalidated row, unreviewed exception, or QA mutation exists.
Final assurance may report lock readiness but must not create P3_SPEC_LOCK or P3_BUILD_LOCK.
forbidden_behaviors: promote P3 without a separate explicit user request
modify repository implementation code in this spec workpack
modify P3-SPEC-COMPILER or earlier approved candidate contents
generate or mutate compiler output fixtures
repair upstream compiler or traceability gaps in place
treat skill text as source authority
ignore invalidation caused by dependency drift
use wall-clock time, local machine time, current user, current branch, or filesystem metadata for reviewed generated_at values
